A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE LINCOLN/KANAWHA COUNTY LINE. IT CONTINUED MOVING TO THE NORTHEAST THROUGH KANAWHA COUNTY AND EVENTUALLY DISSIPATED NEAR THE KANAWHA RIVER IN DOWNTOWN CHARLESTON. THE TORNADO WAS INTERMITTENT AS IT MOVED ALONG ITS PATH. MANY TREES WERE SNAPPED AND/OR UPROOTED. THE SURVEY TEAM ALSO NOTED MINOR ROOF DAMAGE. PRELIMINARY REPORTS SUGGEST THAT THIS TORNADO WAS A EF1 WITH WIND SPEEDS UP TO 90 MPH. IT WAS ON THE GROUND FOR APPROXIMATELY 11 MILES WITH A WIDTH OF 0.2 MILES.

CAMPGROUND AT DRYFORK GREATLY IMPACTED BY FLASH FLOODING. RAPIDLY RISING WATERS SWEPT TRUCK AWAY AT CAMP SITE. ALL ROADS OUT OF CAMPSITE BLOCKED BY MULTIPLE MUDSLIDES, STRANDING THOSE TRYING TO EVACUATE WHILE WATER WAS RISING. NO CELL SERVICE TO CALL FOR HELP. TIME OF EVENT IS AN ESTIMATE.
SEVERAL ROADS CLOSED OVERNIGHT IN HARMAN AREA DUE TO FLASH FLOODING. WATER RESCUES ALSO OCCURRED

MAJOR FLASH FLOODING IMPACTS IN HARMAN. WATER PLANT AND SEWAGE PLANT HAVE BEEN IMPACTED BY FLASH FLOODING AND WERE KNOCKED OFFLINE. NUMEROUS ROADS AND CULVERTS WASHED OUT. SEVERAL HOMES AND/OR TRAILERS PUSHED OFF FOUNDATION. AS OF TEH TIME OF THIS ENTRY, RESCUE WORKERS ARE STILL UNABLE TO GET TO SOME RESIDENTS DUE TO WASHED OUT ROADS
